question: Is the assembly of five or more persons?

yes.question: Is the common object of the assembly to overawe the government or public servant?

no.question: Is the assembly rioting or committing an offence?

no.name: Every member of unlawful assembly guilty of offence committed in prosecution of common object

no.question: Is the assembly of two or more persons?

no.name: Promoting enmity between different groups on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language, etc., and doing acts prejudicial to maintenance of harmony

group: OF OFFENCES AGAINST THE PUBLIC TRANQUILLITY

_meta.chapter_subtype: OF OFFENCES AGAINST THE PUBLIC TRANQUILLITY

sections.name: Every member of unlawful assembly guilty of offence committed in prosecution of common object.
Wantonly giving provocation with intent to cause riot-if rioting be committed; if not committed.
Liability of owner, occupier, etc., of land on which an unlawful assembly or riot takes place.
Assaulting or obstructing public servant when suppressing riot, etc
Promoting enmity between different groups on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language, etc., and doing acts prejudicial to maintenance of harmony
Imputations, assertions prejudicial to national integration.
